edges.description: Impaired LRO trafficking blocks normal maturation of melanosomes in melanocytes and the retinal pigment epithelium.
Impaired LRO trafficking prevents formation of platelet dense (delta) granules.
Defective LRO biogenesis disrupts lamellar bodies (surfactant-storing LROs) in alveolar type II cells, especially in BLOC-3/AP-3 subtypes.
edges.target: Alveolar Type II Cell Lamellar Body Dysfunction

edges.description: Reduced melanin in skin, hair, and eyes manifests as hypopigmentation and ocular albinism.
Impaired dense-granule secretion and aggregation produces variable mucocutaneous and surgical bleeding.
AT2 cell injury triggers an aberrant type 2 innate immune response that amplifies fibrosis.
intermediate_mechanisms: AT2 epithelial injury and CHI3L1 release recruit and activate type 2 innate lymphoid cells.
edges.source: Alveolar Type II Cell Lamellar Body Dysfunction
edges.target: Type 2 Innate Immune Amplification of Fibrosis

edges.description: Sustained fibroblast activation and collagen deposition produce progressive restrictive lung disease.
edges.source: Type 2 Innate Immune Amplification of Fibrosis

nodes.description: In BLOC-3 (HPS-1, HPS-4) and AP-3 (HPS-2) subtypes, defective lamellar-body LRO biogenesis causes alveolar type II cell stress and injury. Histopathology shows vacuolated hyperplastic type II cells with enlarged lamellar bodies and alveolar macrophages with lipofuscin-like deposits, distinguishing HPS-PF from idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis.
nodes.id: Alveolar Type II Cell Lamellar Body Dysfunction

evidence.snippet: Histopathology of HPS pulmonary fibrosis, and not IPF, shows vacuolated hyperplastic type II cells with enlarged lamellar bodies and alveolar macrophages with lipofuscin-like deposits

nodes.description: Pirfenidone and nintedanib are antifibrotic drugs approved for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis but not specifically approved for HPS pulmonary fibrosis; they have prompted trials and off-label consideration. A randomized NIH/NHGRI pirfenidone trial in HPS-PF was stopped for futility at interim analysis.

evidence.snippet: the approval of two new antifibrotic drugs, pirfenidone and nintedanib, has prompted new interest in identifying drugs capable of reversing or halting the progression of HPS-PF

nodes.description: Prolonged bleeding from platelet dense-granule (delta granule) deficiency, causing variable bruising, epistaxis, gingival bleeding, postpartum hemorrhage, and prolonged bleeding with menses or after surgery.

evidence.snippet: The bleeding diathesis can result in variable degrees of bruising, epistaxis, gingival bleeding, postpartum hemorrhage

nodes.description: Granulomatous colitis in HPS is managed with corticosteroids as a first-line anti-inflammatory agent.

evidence.snippet: steroids, other anti-inflammatory agents, and/or Remicade® for granulomatous colitis

nodes.description: HPS results from defective biogenesis of lysosome-related organelles (LROs) such as melanosomes, platelet dense granules, and the lamellar bodies of alveolar type II cells. The causative genes encode subunits of multi-protein complexes (the AP-3 adaptor complex and the BLOC-1, BLOC-2, and BLOC-3 complexes) that direct cargo trafficking from early endosomes to maturing LROs. Loss of any subunit impairs LRO formation, producing the shared multisystem phenotype.

evidence.snippet: the HPS proteins play an essential role in the biogenesis and function of lysosome-related organelles (LROs) in alveolar epithelial type II (AT2) cells
characterised by abnormal biogenesis of lysosome-related organelles, manifests with oculocutaneous albinism and excessive bleeding of variable severity

nodes.description: Failure to mature melanosomes in melanocytes and the retinal pigment epithelium reduces melanin production, producing oculocutaneous albinism with the characteristic ocular features (nystagmus, foveal hypoplasia, reduced visual acuity).

evidence.snippet: characterized by oculocutaneous albinism (OCA), bleeding diathesis, immunodeficiency, granulomatous colitis, and pulmonary fibrosis

nodes.description: DDAVP (desmopressin acetate) is used to support hemostasis for procedures such as wisdom tooth extraction and other invasive procedures in HPS.

evidence.snippet: DDAVP® (desmopressin acetate) for wisdom tooth extraction and invasive procedures

nodes.description: Inflammatory bowel disease resembling Crohn disease; severe in about 15% of affected individuals.

evidence.snippet: Granulomatous colitis is severe in about 15% of affected individuals

nodes.description: Infliximab (Remicade), an anti-TNF monoclonal antibody, is used for HPS-associated granulomatous colitis refractory to other anti-inflammatory agents.

evidence.snippet: steroids, other anti-inflammatory agents, and/or Remicade® for granulomatous colitis

nodes.description: Lung transplantation is the only potentially life-prolonging treatment for end-stage HPS pulmonary fibrosis.

evidence.snippet: lung transplantation remains the only potentially life-prolonging treatment

nodes.description: Hypopigmentation of skin, hair, and eyes due to defective melanosome biogenesis in melanocytes and retinal pigment epithelium. Hair ranges from white to brown and skin from white to olive, usually lighter than unaffected family members.

evidence.snippet: Hermansky-Pudlak syndrome (HPS) is characterized by oculocutaneous albinism, a bleeding diathesis

nodes.description: Absence of platelet dense (delta) granules removes the stored pool of ADP, ATP, serotonin, and calcium needed for the platelet secretion and second-wave aggregation response, producing a storage-pool bleeding diathesis. Absent dense bodies on whole-mount electron microscopy is the diagnostic hallmark.

evidence.snippet: demonstration of absence of platelet delta granules (dense bodies) on electron microscopy

nodes.description: Platelet or red blood cell transfusions are used for surgery or protracted bleeding; HLA-matched single-donor platelets are used as needed.

evidence.snippet: platelet or red blood cell transfusions for surgery or protracted bleeding; HLA-matched single-donor platelets as needed

nodes.description: Progressive restrictive lung disease seen mainly in HPS-1, HPS-2, and HPS-4. Typically causes symptoms in the early 30s and can progress to death within a decade. In HPS-1 it is essentially fully penetrant.

evidence.snippet: HPS pulmonary fibrosis (HPS-PF) occurs in 100% of patients with subtype HPS-1 and has a similar presentation to idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis
Pulmonary fibrosis is highly prevalent in three out of 10 genetic types of HPS (HPS-1, HPS-2 and HPS-4)

nodes.description: A CHI3L1-CRTH2 axis recruits and activates type 2 innate lymphoid cells (ILC2s) in HPS lungs. ILC2s stimulate fibroblast proliferation and differentiation, partly via amphiregulin-EGFR signaling, amplifying the fibrotic response after epithelial injury.
nodes.id: Type 2 Innate Immune Amplification of Fibrosis

evidence.snippet: ILC2s may directly stimulate the proliferation and differentiation of primary lung fibroblasts partially through amphiregulin-EGFR-dependent mechanisms
